title: Zanthoxylum macrophyllum
intro: Zanthoxylum macrophyllum, also known as the large-leaved prickly ash, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Zanthoxylum macrophyllum include lignans, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, decoctions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into salves and creams for topical application.
taxonomy: Zanthoxylum macrophyllum is classified within the family Rutaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as citrus or rue family. This family comprises about 160 genera and over 1,000 species, including many well-known plants such as citrus and pomegranate. The genus Zanthoxylum is a large and diverse group with over 200 species, primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions of the world. Further research is needed to confirm the exact classification and distribution of Zanthoxylum macrophyllum.
uses: Zanthoxylum macrophyllum is a plant species that has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties.
